methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $93M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Cognichip is a Series A chip and semiconductor company that has raised $93M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 8 private peers in the sector—including Etched, Substrate, Positron AI, and Rain AI at similar or adjacent stages—as well as 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the $93M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying $186M. Peer-median triangulation across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.3x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied; Cognichip's revenue is not on file, which is standard for private companies at this stage.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $158M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $593M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Cognichip will command peer valuations at the lower end of the comparable set (Rain AI at $500M) or track higher-stage Series B benchmarks (Etched at $5.0B). Revenue traction, product differentiation, and path to margin will be critical to resolving this uncertainty at the next financing.
